name: LaunchDarkly
what_it_does: Enterprise feature flag management platform with REST API for creating, toggling, and targeting feature flags across environments, supporting gradual rollouts, A/B testing, and kill switches.
use_cases: Agents dynamically enabling or disabling features in response to incidents or deployments
CI/CD automation toggling flags as part of progressive delivery pipelines
Reading flag configurations to understand what features are enabled for a given user/environment
Scheduling flag rollouts and targeting rule changes via API
Experiment management — creating and reading A/B test configurations programmatically
not_for: Simple on/off feature flags without targeting (use a free/OSS tool like Unleash or Flipt)
Config management beyond boolean/string/number flags (use a dedicated config service)
Applications with extremely low latency requirements for flag evaluation (use local SDK)
Teams that need fully self-hosted feature flagging (use Unleash or Flipt OSS)
best_when: You need enterprise-grade feature management with advanced targeting, experimentation, and audit trails, and want a managed SaaS solution with deep SDK support across all major languages.
avoid_when: Your use case is simple on/off flags without targeting, or your team's budget doesn't justify enterprise pricing.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens and service tokens available. OAuth2 for third-party integrations. Tokens can be scoped to read-only or specific resource types. Role-based access control available on enterprise plans.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: up to 1,000 MAUs, limited flags, no experimentation
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $10/month; Business and Enterprise with custom pricing
pricing.notes: Free tier is limited but usable for small projects. Experimentation and advanced targeting require paid plans. Pricing scales with monthly active users (MAUs).

performance.rate_limits: REST API: 429 Too Many Requests enforced; SDK streaming connections are long-lived

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented REST API with OpenAPI spec. Error responses include status codes and descriptive messages. Rate limit headers returned on all responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Flag reads are idempotent. Flag updates use patch operations (JSON Patch) — must be aware of concurrent modification. Flag creation is not idempotent by default.
known_agent_gotchas: Flag updates use JSON Patch format — agents must construct proper patch documents, not simple PUT bodies
Environment keys are required for all flag operations — easy to accidentally target wrong environment
SDK evaluation and REST API management are separate surfaces — SDK is for eval, API is for config
Webhook payloads include old/new state but not full flag config — may need secondary API call for context
Rate limits are enforced per API token — shared tokens across agents can hit limits
Flag targeting rules use LaunchDarkly's rule DSL — complex targeting requires understanding their schema
